Title: BAHD Company: The Ever-Expanding Roles of the BAHD Acyltransferase Gene Family in Plants

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: reduces water loss; contributes to water repellency, light reflection, particle adhesion, herbivore resistance, and pollen coat formation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for production of >C28 cuticular wax components; loss causes glossy, wax-deficient phenotype
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtCER2 is a characterized clade 2 BAHD implicated in epicuticular wax biosynthesis. 2. Arabidopsis cer2 mutants lack >C28 cuticular wax components and display a glossy phenotype. 3. CER2-like BAHDs are important for adaptation to dry conditions and proper pollen coat formation.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: drought conditions influence male sterility phenotype
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review paper summarizes CER2-like proteins as noncanonical BAHDs involved in very long chain fatty acid elongation rather than a directly demonstrated small-molecule acyltransferase reaction.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: loss causes wax deficiency and drought-associated male sterility
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful target for engineering cuticle properties, drought adaptation, and wax composition in crops

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: limits water loss and affects surface physiological properties
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: involved in epicuticular wax biosynthesis; complements Arabidopsis cer2 loss of function
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmGlossy2 is a clade 2 BAHD associated with epicuticular wax production in maize. 2. It complements AtCER2 loss of function in Arabidopsis, supporting conserved function. 3. It contributes to very long chain lipid composition of the cuticle.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Some differences in very long chain fatty acyl lipids suggest functional diversification relative to ZmGlossy2-like.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: mutant glossy phenotype implies altered cuticle performance
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate for modifying wax composition and drought-related cuticle traits in cereals

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: implicated in epicuticular wax biosynthesis and able to complement AtCER2 function
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmGlossy2-like is a second maize CER2-related BAHD. 2. It complements Arabidopsis cer2 mutants, indicating conserved wax-related function. 3. Differences in lipid profiles indicate diversification from ZmGlossy2.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review highlights partial functional diversification despite complementation ability.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: may provide alternative alleles for tuning wax composition

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: protective barrier against water loss, salt/osmotic stress, and pathogens
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for proper incorporation of 9(10),16-dihydroxy-hexadecanoic acid into cutin; mutants show stress-sensitive cuticle defects
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DCR-like BAHDs are implicated in cutin assembly. 2. Arabidopsis dcr-like mutants fail to properly incorporate a key dihydroxy fatty acid into cutin. 3. Mutants display severe phenotypes under water deprivation, high salt, and osmotic stress.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: water deprivation; high salt; osmotic stress
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Exact catalytic activity remains uncertain; review suggests possible acyl-exchange role to supply substrate for CYP77A6.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: loss leads to stress susceptibility due to defective cuticle
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: potential target to improve cuticle integrity and abiotic stress tolerance

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pollen wall formation and male fertility
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: transfers hydroxycinnamoyl groups to ω-hydroxy fatty acids; mutant shows complete male sterility and abnormal anther cuticle
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant phenotype and biochemical transferase evidence
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Rice DPW2 encodes a BAHD required for pollen wall/anther cuticle formation. 2. It transfers hydroxycinnamoyl groups to ω-hydroxy fatty acids in tapetum. 3. Mutation causes complete male sterility.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Role links BAHD activity to sporopollenin biosynthesis in reproductive tissues.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: important fertility-related target; manipulation requires caution because reduced activity compromises pollen viability

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase family; clade 5; HXXXD catalytic motif
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: hydroxycinnamoyl shikimate esters; hydroxycinnamoyl quinate esters
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: transfers hydroxycinnamoyl moieties from hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA donors to shikimate and also quinate acceptors
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms central intermediates leading to lignin and other phenylpropanoids
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point/central enzyme in monolignol and phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: cell wall/apoplast for lignin; broader phenylpropanoid pools in tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports plant growth, vascular development, and terrestrial adaptation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: defines formation of hydroxycinnamoyl shikimate/quinate intermediates in phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene identification plus biochemical enzyme assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NtHCT was the first described HST gene sequence. 2. It accepts shikimate and also quinate with differently substituted hydroxycinnamoyl-CoAs. 3. HCT/HST enzymes are central to general phenylpropanoid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Many HCTs also catalyze reverse reactions and can cleave hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA to free acids and CoA.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: central engineering node for lignin and hydroxycinnamate-derived nutraceuticals, but perturbation can cause growth defects
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: reduced HCT/HST activity can cause dwarfism and lignin defects

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: hydroxycinnamoyl shikimate; chlorogenic acid and related caffeoylquinate esters
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: transfers hydroxycinnamoyl groups to shikimate and/or quinate; some enzymes also catalyze reverse reactions and low-level dicaffeoylquinate formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursors for caffeate derivatives, monolignols, and chlorogenic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: various plant tissues; lignin in cell walls
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: UV protection, structural support, stress adaptation, and nutraceutical value
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: participates in multiple steps of phenylpropanoid biosynthesis and underpins chlorogenate and monolignol production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: enzyme assays, structural biology, mutant/complementation studies
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HST/HQT activities are broadly conserved across land plants. 2. They are core BAHD activities participating in multiple steps of the general phenylpropanoid pathway. 3. Their products feed lignin, chlorogenate, and other hydroxycinnamate-derived metabolites.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: low pH favors chlorogenate:chlorogenate activity in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review-level synthesis across species rather than one focal gene; included because HST/HQT are primary summarized BAHD activities central to nutrient-relevant phenolics.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: prominent engineering targets for chlorogenic acid and lignin modification
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: silencing can impair growth and alter lignin composition

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: transfers 4-coumaroyl group from 4-coumaroyl-CoA to shikimate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms central intermediate required for G and S lignin unit biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: structural support and adaptation to land
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: loss/silencing causes dwarfism and drastic lignin composition changes with increased H/S ratio effects
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA insertion, RNA silencing, crystal structure, mutagenesis, complementation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtHCT is a central BAHD in phenylpropanoid and lignin biosynthesis. 2. Structural analyses identified Arg356 and catalytic His153 as crucial for acceptor binding/catalysis. 3. Reduced HCT activity causes dwarfism and altered lignin composition.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Nonseed plant HST orthologs can complement Arabidopsis HCT loss, indicating deep conservation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: important but sensitive target for lignin engineering; partial modulation preferable to avoid severe growth defects

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: forms hydroxycinnamoyl esters contributing to ancestral phenylpropanoid/cuticle chemistry
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies intermediates for cuticle-associated phenolics and conserved lignin-pathway chemistry
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: intact cuticle formation and land-plant adaptation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: important for caffeoylthreonate ester biosynthesis and intact cuticle formation; complements Arabidopsis HCT mutant function
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpHST has conserved HST function across land plants. 2. It complements Arabidopsis HCT mutants. 3. In moss, it is also important for caffeoylthreonate esters linked to cuticle integrity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlights ancestral BAHD role beyond lignin in nonvascular plants.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: illustrates transferable conserved HCT functions for engineering phenylpropanoid flux

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes hydroxycinnamoyl transfer to shikimate in a conserved phenylpropanoid step
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms intermediate for lignin/phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: structural support and adaptation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: conserved HCT function supported by structural analysis and complementation-related observations
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: structural biology and comparative functional analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmHCT preserves key structural features of HST/HCT enzymes. 2. Comparative evidence supports conservation of HCT acceptor-binding logic. 3. Nonseed plant HCTs can substitute for Arabidopsis HCT function.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a summarized core conserved HCT representative in the review.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: supports broad phylogenetic portability of HCT-like activities

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: quinate/shikimate ester formation; dicaffeoylquinate side reaction
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA; quinate; shikimate; chlorogenate in side reaction
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: transfers hydroxycinnamoyl groups to quinate/shikimate and can catalyze low-level chlorogenate-dependent dicaffeoylquinate formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms chlorogenic acid precursors and related esters
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: coffee tissues rich in chlorogenic acids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antioxidant and nutraceutical phenolics
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: structural determinants govern shikimate/quinate discrimination and enhanced dicaffeoylquinate formation in specific mutants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: crystal structure, docking, mutagenesis, biochemical assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Coffee CcaHCT was the first crystal structure from clade 5. 2. His153 is essential, and Leu400/Phe402 help determine shikimate versus quinate preference. 3. Mutation of His154 increases 3,5-dicaffeoylquinate formation.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: low pH and low CoA favor chlorogenate:chlorogenate activity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GFP fusion excluded vacuolar localization despite low-pH chlorogenate transfer activity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: valuable scaffold for engineering chlorogenic acid and dicaffeoylquinate production

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: transfers 4-coumaroyl moiety to shikimate via catalytic His162-mediated mechanism
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms direct intermediate for downstream caffeoylshikimate/lignin branch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: structural support
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: strong substrate discrimination against quinate and defined catalytic residues governing shikimate acylation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: apo and ternary crystal structures; alanine mutagenesis; calorimetry
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbHCT structures revealed substrate-induced closure of binding pockets. 2. His162 and Arg371 are essential for catalysis and substrate binding. 3. Pro32 contributes to discrimination against quinate.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Calorimetry indicated donor CoA ester binds before acceptor substrate.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: informative for structure-guided engineering of HCT specificity in grasses

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes shikimate acylation analogous to sorghum HCT
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms precursor for lignin phenylpropanoid branch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell wall structure
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: structural work supports conserved HCT catalytic logic in grasses
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvHCT2a showed structural features similar to SbHCT. 2. It supports conservation of HST catalytic mechanism in grasses. 3. It is part of the central lignin-related BAHD toolkit.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as representative HCT structural evidence in the review.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: relevant for cell wall engineering in bioenergy grasses

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: acylates quinate with hydroxycinnamoyl donor; no activity detected with many other tested acceptors
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: suggests ancestral ability to make quinate esters related to preland-plant phenylpropanoid chemistry
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: suggested role in ancestral phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports idea that ancestral BAHDs acylated quinate-like substrates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The C. braunii enzyme acylated quinate among diverse tested acceptors. 2. It was designated CbHQT-like. 3. It supports an ancestral origin of HQT/HST-like chemistry before land plants.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Charophytic algae lack true lignin but possess prelignin/phenylpropanoid-related chemistry.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful for evolutionary enzyme discovery rather than direct breeding

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: ω-hydroxy fatty acids and/or fatty alcohols; hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: transfers hydroxycinnamoyl moiety to ω-hydroxyacids/fatty alcohols to generate cutin-related monomers
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms cutin/suberin monomers
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: protective barrier important for terrestrial adaptation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: involved in cutin monomer biosynthesis and broadly conserved in land plants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MeHFT is a clade 6 BAHD involved in cutin monomer biosynthesis. 2. This activity is broadly conserved and likely essential. 3. It supports the role of BAHDs in land-plant protective barrier evolution.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review links this subclade with conserved barrier polymer biochemistry.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: potential target for barrier polymer engineering and stress resilience

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: transfers feruloyl moieties onto aliphatic acceptors for barrier polymer monomer formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms barrier polymer monomers
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports protective barrier formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: involved in cutin monomer biosynthesis in early land plants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MpFHT is a characterized clade 6 feruloyltransferase. 2. It participates in biosynthesis of cutin-related monomers. 3. It illustrates evolutionary conservation of BAHD roles in protective barrier assembly.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Grouped with MeHFT as broadly conserved cutin-associated BAHDs.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate for reconstructing ancestral barrier polymer chemistry

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: transfers ferulate moiety to ω-hydroxy fatty acids in suberin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms suberin monomers
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: links suberin to cell wall and contributes to protective barrier properties
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: loss strongly reduces ferulate monomers in root and seed coat suberin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutants and biochemical enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtASFT catalyzes transfer of ferulate to ω-hydroxy fatty acids. 2. Knockout reduces ferulate monomers in root and seed coat suberin. 3. It is a key BAHD for suberin phenolic modification.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: root and seed coat-associated suberizing tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: root and seed coat-associated suberizing tissues
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ferulate esters are proposed to link suberin with the cell wall via C-C and ether bonds.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: engineering target for root barrier properties and stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: altered suberin composition may affect transport/barrier properties

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: promotes incorporation of ferulate modifications into suberin and cutin-related polyesters
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms modified barrier polymer monomers
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: barrier integrity and salt stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ectopic expression in Arabidopsis increased ferulate modifications in suberin and leaf cutin and increased salt stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtFHT1 promotes ferulate modifications in root suberin and leaf cutin. 2. Ectopic expression in Arabidopsis increased these modifications. 3. The resulting polyester changes enhanced salt stress tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review cites homolog support for ASFT-like activities in woody species.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ectopic expression promoter not specified in review
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: promising target to improve abiotic stress tolerance via barrier polymer remodeling

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: feruloyl-CoA; coumaryl alcohol; coniferyl alcohol; sinapyl alcohol
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: transfers feruloyl group specifically from feruloyl-CoA to canonical monolignols
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms acylated monolignol precursors incorporated into lignin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: creates alkali-labile lignin ester bonds and improves saccharification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: stable expression in poplar led to incorporation of feruloylated monolignols into lignin and increased saccharification yield after mild alkaline treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant enzyme assay; stable transformation; lignin analysis; saccharification assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AsFMT specifically uses feruloyl-CoA and all three monolignols as acceptors. 2. In transgenic poplar, its products are incorporated into lignin via labile ester bonds. 3. Increased feruloylated conjugates improve saccharification yield.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: mild alkaline treatment for saccharification assessment
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: An example of dicot monolignol acyltransferase, whereas many related enzymes are monocot-specific clade 6 members.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: excellent target for biomass engineering to improve deconstruction without major performance penalty
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: review notes no negative overall plant performance in cited manipulations

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: likely UDP-arabinofuranose-related acceptor and hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA donor (mechanistic model); exact in vivo substrate not fully established in review
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: changes hydroxycinnamate decoration of grass cell wall arabinoxylan
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: modifies polymer-associated hydroxycinnamate content affecting digestibility
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: affects biomass digestibility and recalcitrance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: overexpression drastically changed cell wall ferulate and 4-coumarate content of glucuronoarabinoxylan
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Rice OsAT10 alters ferulate and 4-coumarate substitution of glucuronoarabinoxylan. 2. Reduction of ferulate substitutions is expected to improve digestibility of grass cell walls. 3. It is a promising biomass engineering BAHD.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Exact acceptor substrate in vivo remains debated; review discusses UDP-arabinofuranose acylation model.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: overexpression promoter not specified in review
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful for forage/biofuel breeding to lower recalcitrance

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: transfers hydroxycinnamoyl groups onto spermidine, potentially multiple times
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms pollen wall-associated phenolamides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: implicated in pollen wall formation and UV protection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: loss-of-function suggests cinnamoyl spermidine derivatives are involved in pollen wall formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtSHT produces hydroxycinnamoyl spermidine derivatives. 2. Mutant phenotypes implicate these metabolites in pollen wall formation. 3. BAHD-dependent phenolamides likely contribute to reproductive stress resilience.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Clade 5 N-HCTs can acylate primary and secondary amines and generate multiply substituted spermidines/spermines.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: potential fertility-related phenolamide engineering target, but caution due to reproductive role
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: mutation may compromise pollen wall integrity

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: transfers hydroxycinnamoyl group to agmatine and related amines
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursors of hordatines
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antifungal defense
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: involved in biosynthesis of antifungal hordatines in barley seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HvACT is a clade 4 agmatine coumaroyltransferase. 2. It contributes to hordatine biosynthesis in barley seedlings. 3. Structural analysis showed catalytic His152 is crucial for activity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalytic His152Ala causes severe activity loss; structurally resembles O-HCTs.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate for boosting phenolamide-mediated disease resistance

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: transfers hydroxycinnamoyl group to agmatine and related amine acceptors
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms phenolamide defense metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: defense-related phenolamides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: encodes a structurally characterized agmatine coumaroyltransferase; Phe39 orientation differs from HvACT
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaACT1 is a clade 4 N-acylating BAHD. 2. Structural comparison with HvACT revealed differences in the hydroxycinnamoyl-binding pocket. 3. It supports diversification among cereal agmatine coumaroyltransferases.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review notes many clade 4 enzymes use diverse amine acceptors and donor CoA esters despite annotations.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful for engineering cereal phenolamide chemistry

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: putrescine and related polyamines; hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA donors
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: transfers hydroxycinnamoyl groups to putrescine and other polyamines
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms defense-related phenolamides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: response to biotic and abiotic stresses; antiherbivore/phytoalexin roles
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: plays a role in phenolamide biosynthesis in response to multiple biotic and abiotic stresses
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NaAT1 is a clade 4 putrescine hydroxycinnamoyltransferase. 2. It contributes to phenolamide accumulation. 3. Its role is linked to multiple biotic and abiotic stress responses.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review uses NaAT1 as an example of ecological relevance of BAHD-derived phenolamides.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate to strengthen inducible defense phenolamides
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: possible metabolic cost associated with defense activation

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: sequential sucrose acylation in glandular trichomes
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: sucrose or partially acylated sucroses; acyl-CoAs of varying chain lengths
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: sequentially add acyl chains to specific hydroxyls of sugar cores, chiefly sucrose
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms acylsugar end products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: type I/IV trichome tip cells on leaf and stem surfaces
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: insect defense and possibly desiccation tolerance; root-exuded forms affect rhizobiome recruitment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: core pathway enzymes generating acylsugar diversity across Solanaceae
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: biochemical assays, comparative evolutionary analysis, metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ASATs form the core acylsugar pathway in Solanaceae trichomes. 2. Different species carry variable ASAT complements and rapidly evolving substrate specificities. 3. These enzymes underlie major acylsugar structural diversity with ecological defense functions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: type I/IV trichome tip cells on leaf and stem surfaces
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: acylhydrolases; invertases (downstream modifiers in some species)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review synthesizes the ASAT module as a major BAHD innovation rather than a single-gene study.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: high-value targets for enhancing natural insect defense in Solanaceae
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: altered trichome metabolite profiles may affect ecological interactions and potentially fruit/leaf surface properties

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: adds the second acyl chain to monoacylated sucrose
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms direct precursor for more highly acylated acylsugars
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: trichome tip cells on aerial surfaces
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: insect defense and desiccation tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: uses monoacylated sucrose in cultivated tomato; orthologs in wild relatives show expanded promiscuity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative enzyme assays and phylogeny-guided amino acid analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SIASAT2 catalyzes the second acylation step on monoacylated sucrose. 2. Orthologs in wild relatives acquired broader activity toward mono- and/or diacylsucroses. 3. Three amino acid changes explain major shifts in substrate promiscuity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promiscuity differs among Peruvian populations of S. pennellii.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: excellent target for tuning acylsugar chemotypes via precise amino acid editing
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: chemotype changes may alter ecological interactions

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ASAT3 branch controlling pyranose versus furanose ring acylation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: adds acyl chains either to furanose or pyranose ring depending on variant
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly diversifies acylsugar structures
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ecological defense chemistry diversification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: duplication and divergence created ancestral furanose-ring activity and novel pyranose-ring activity, driving pathway reorganization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: phylogeny-guided enzyme analysis and comparative biochemistry
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ASAT3 duplicated in the ancestor of S. pennellii and S. habrochaites. 2. ShASAT3-F retains ancestral furanose-ring acylation while ShASAT3-P/SpASAT3-P perform novel pyranose-ring acylation. 3. These changes reorganized acylsugar pathways and expanded chemotype diversity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Population-specific amino acid changes correlate with Andean migration and chemotype evolution.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: strong candidates for reprogramming acylsugar regiospecificity in crop tomato
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ecological tradeoffs through altered defense blend

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: putatively catalyzes terminal N-acylation of vanillylamine with variable-length fatty acyl-CoAs
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms capsaicinoid end products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antimicrobial defense and deterrence of mammals while allowing bird dispersal
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: genetic studies support essential role in capsaicinoid accumulation/pungency
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AT3/Pun1 is genetically linked to capsaicinoid accumulation. 2. It is assigned as the terminal capsaicin synthase BAHD. 3. Direct biochemical proof is still lacking because the protein is highly insoluble.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: environmental moisture influences ecological selection on pungency
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review explicitly cautions that other non-BAHD enzymes with capsaicin synthase activity cannot be entirely excluded in absence of biochemical data.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: major locus for pungency breeding, but biochemical uncertainty warrants caution
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: changes in pungency affect ecology, seed dispersal interactions, and pathogen defense

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: transfers hydroxycinnamoyl units to aliphatic hydroxyl groups of phenyllactate derivatives; shows substrate promiscuity and can form esters and amides
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms rosmarinic acid scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: nutraceutical/antioxidant phenolic
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: catalyzes key acylation step in rosmarinic acid biosynthesis and supports synthetic biology production of rosmarinate-related compounds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: biochemical characterization; crystal structure; heterologous pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CbRAS transfers hydroxycinnamoyl moieties to hydroxyphenyllactate derivatives. 2. It is promiscuous and catalyzes both ester and amide formation. 3. It is a central BAHD used for rosmarinate synthetic biology.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Arg handle of HST is replaced by Lys with equivalent acceptor-binding role.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: strong candidate for microbial and plant engineering of rosmarinic/salvianolic acids

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: rosmarinic acid leading to salvianolic acid B
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes rosmarinate-forming BAHD step in reconstructed pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms rosmarinic acid precursor to salvianolic acid B
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pharmaceutical/nutraceutical phenolic
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enabled de novo salvianolic acid B production in yeast when included in reconstructed rosmarinate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmRAS is required in reconstructed rosmarinate biosynthesis. 2. Yeast using SmRAS and endogenous substitute steps produced salvianolic acid B. 3. It demonstrates BAHD utility in synthetic biology.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review cites de novo production of 30 μg/L salvianolic acid B in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful for microbial production of medicinal phenolics
